item.title: Sycophancy resurfaces in JS
item.description: A trend of spending parliamentary time on sycophancy has begun to reappear in the opening session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad, reflecting the pattern seen in previous parliaments....

item.title: Diplomatic efforts on to bring back Banglar Joyjatra: minister
item.description: Shipping minister Shaikh Rabiul Alam said on Saturday that the government was using all possible diplomatic channels to bring back Banglar Joyjatra, a Bangladesh Shipping Corporation vessel stranded in the Strait of Hormuz....

item.title: MEASLES OUTBREAK: Doctors call for public health emergency
item.description: A platform of doctors, public health experts, and rights activists on Saturday urged the government to declare a public health emergency amid the ongoing measles outbreak across Bangladesh....

item.title: Long JS boycott to harm democracy: speaker
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad speaker retired Major Hafiz Uddin Ahmed, Bir Bikram on Saturday said that prolonged boycotts of parliament would undermine democracy, although walkouts remain a legitimate right of the opposition....

item.title: Dhaka for revival of SAARC
item.description: Dhaka on Saturday at a ministerial panel during a diplomacy forum in Türkiye said that revival of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation was one of its major foreign policy objectives....

item.title: Leftist leaders in Spain to rally against far right
item.description: Leftist leaders from around the globe met in Barcelona on Saturday to rally against the threat to democracy from the far right, with Spanish prime minister Pedro Sanchez and Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum displaying closer ties......

item.title: Israel using security as pretext for land grab: Turkey
item.description: Turkey’s top diplomat on Saturday accused Israel of creating an international ‘illusion’ and using security concerns as a pretext to seize ‘more land,’ in the latest flare‑up in escalating tensions between the two regional powers......

item.title: France blames Hezbollah for peacekeeper’s death
item.description: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded in an attack Saturday on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon that appeared to have been carried out by Hezbollah, French president Emmanuel Macron said......

item.title: Croatians protest seeking higher wages, pensions
item.description: Thousands of Croatians protested Saturday in the capital Zagreb to demand higher salaries and pensions amid rising inflation in the EU nation in recent months......

item.title: Europe’s far right to rally over immigration in Milan
item.description: Far-right leaders from Europe gather in Milan Saturday for a rally against irregular immigration and Brussels bureaucracy, the first since the electoral defeat of nationalist Viktor Orban in Hungary......

item.title: Bone fragments found in Mexico City ahead of WC
item.description: More than 1,000 bone fragments have been found near a lake in Mexico City, authorities and a volunteer group said, just weeks before it hosts the World Cup—another grim reminder of the country’s violent drug war......

item.title: Pope regrets remarks seen as Trump debate
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said Saturday he regretted remarks he made were interpreted as a response to criticism from president Donald Trump, insisting he had no interest in debating the US leader......

item.title: Pakistan leaders return after Iran diplomacy
item.description: Pakistan’s powerful military chief and prime minister on Saturday concluded separate visits aimed at ending the Iran war, with Field Marshal Asim Munir leaving Tehran and premier Shehbaz Sharif headed home from Turkey......

item.title: New Zealand rejects China airspace accusation
item.description: New Zealand rejected on Saturday Beijing’s allegations of ‘disruptive and irresponsible’ military surveillance in Chinese airspace and insisted its manoeuvres were part of UN-backed efforts to enforce sanctions against North Korea......

item.title: Greek deputy minister quits in qualifications row
item.description: Greek deputy agriculture and food minister Makarios Lazaridis resigned Saturday, following accusations that he was unqualified and had only been nominated because of close connections to the ruling conservatives......

item.title: Implement referendum verdict immediately: Jamaat-led alliance
item.description: Leaders of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance on Saturday called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to take immediate steps to implement the February 12 referendum verdict on reforms in line with the July National Charter....

item.title: Agri minister calls for action if fertiliser price charged higher
item.description: Agriculture, fisheries and livestock minister Mohammed Aminur Rashid on Saturday emphasised financial solvency of farmers to strengthen country’s economy. ...

item.title: Bangladesh ‘hostage’ to IMF terms: Titumir
item.description: Rashed Al Mahmud Titumir, adviser to the prime minister Tarique Rahman, on Saturday said that Bangladesh has become ‘hostage’ to the loan conditions of the International Monetary Fund and now has to comply with international agreements......

item.title: US extends sanctions waiver
item.description: US president Donald Trump’s administration on Friday issued a month-long sanctions waiver allowing the sale of Russian oil and petroleum products that are at sea, extending an earlier move to soften surging energy prices......

item.title: Bobby Hajjaj, Ziauddin Hyder visit Daffodil Robotics showroom
item.description: State minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj and adviser to prime minister Ziauddin Hyder have recently visited the Daffodil Robotics showroom in Dhaka....Geographic Reference

item.title: Knowledge-driven transformation in retail sector stressed
item.description: Industry leaders at the 8th Bangladesh Retail Congress 2026 in Dhaka on Saturday underscored that data-driven innovation and consumer-centric technology were now the primary engines for sustainable growth in the nation’s rapidly evolving retail landscape......

item.title: DCCI, China chambers eye strong ties
item.description: Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry signed a Memorandum of Cooperation with three leading Chinese Chambers on Saturday to enhance the bilateral trade relations and expand business cooperation between entrepreneurs of Bangladesh and China......

item.title: Japan stocks dealers extend cooperation
item.description: The DSE Brokers Association of Bangladesh has signed a memorandum of understanding with the Japan Securities Dealers Association (JSDA) aimed at fostering sustainable development, improving efficiency and strengthening international cooperation in Bangladesh’s capital market......

item.title: Khosru holds meeting with IMF
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has categorically reiterated that it is a development partner of Bangladesh and expressed its willingness to continue supporting the country during the tenure of this democratically elected government......

item.title: MDB heads underscore importance of deeper collaboration
item.description: In a period of heightened global uncertainty, including the evolving situation in the Middle East, Heads of Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) have underscored the importance of close cooperation to support stability, safeguard development progress, and respond to mounting pressures in their member economies......

item.title: ‘Waves of Colors’ festival features artists from 27 countries
item.description: A three-day international watercolour festival titled ‘Waves of Colors’ began at the Nalinikanta Bhattashali Gallery of the Bangladesh National Museum on Saturtday....

item.title: Iran says Strait is open as Trump spins
item.description: IRAN announced Friday that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping after a ceasefire was declared in Lebanon......

item.title: Starting with the toilet
item.description: THE smell hits first. In a district hospital just outside Dhaka, a patient’s family member searches for a working toilet. The door hangs half-broken; inside, there is no running water, no soap, and no record of when it was last cleaned. A nurse shrugs, maintenance is ‘someone else’s job.’ Down the corridor, a cleaner from a historically marginalised Harijan community finishes......Geographic Reference

item.title: Brick economy behind air crisis
item.description: AIR pollution crisis is often framed through traffic congestion, seasonal smog or industrial emissions. Yet, one of its most persistent and structurally embedded drivers lies in the country’s construction economy. As urban expansion accelerates and infrastructure demand rises, fired clay bricks remain the dominant building material. This dependence has turned brick......

item.title: Managing tides in south-west
item.description: A TRAGEDY is quietly unfolding in Bangladesh’s south-west. The Water Development Board has for decades treated the rivers like clogged pipes that need ‘cleaning’ through expensive structural engineering model, including mechanical dredging. Yet, as any resident near the Kobadak or Hari rivers will say, the silt returns almost as fast as the machines can remove it......

item.title: Politics of violence, slander undermines democracy
item.description: MAINSTREAMpoliticalculture, marked by violent polarisation and a lack of appetite for debating differences, formed the backdrop to the July uprising and the demand for reform. Yet, the politics of slander and retaliation persists. Verbal abuse and violence over the untoward remarks on the prime minister by the Jatiya Ganatantrik Party vice-president suggest that the......Politics

item.title: Delay in ACC appointment hurts accountability
item.description: THE Anti-Corruption Commission, the principal public agency that fights against corruption, has been practically inactive since March 3, when the chair and two other commissioners stepped down two weeks after the Bangladesh Nationalist Party assumed office following the February 12 general elections. In the absence of the commission, important anti-corruption......

item.title: Australia, Japan ink warship deal
item.description: Japan agreed on Saturday on a deal to provide Australia’s navy with the first of almost a dozen stealth frigates, part of a wider military build up by Canberra aimed at boosting its long-range firepower to deter China....

item.title: Thousands stage protests in Germany
item.description: Thousands of people demonstrated across Germany on Saturday, urging a faster shift to renewable energy and accusing conservative chancellor Friedrich Merz’s coalition of putting the brakes on the transition....

item.title: PM’s foreign adviser presents new PPP model in Turkiye
item.description: Prime minister’s foreign affairs adviser Humayun Kabir has unveiled a new Public-Private Partnership model at the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, stressing a shift from traditional aid to capacity building, skills development and technology transfer....

item.title: No case filed over killing two in mob beating
item.description: No case was filed till Saturday night over the killing of two men in mob beating in Manikganj, while a case was filed against five people with the Manikganj Sadar police on Saturday over the death of a nine-year old girl....

item.title: Rajshahi Nursing College students demand principal appointment
item.description: Students of Rajshahi Nursing College on Saturday staged a sit-in demanding immediate appointment of a principal at the college, alleging academic and administrative deadlock....

item.title: Establishment of freedom of expression demanded
item.description: Discussants at a roundtable in Dhaka on Saturday demanded that human rights and freedom of expression for all people must be ensured, saying that those having opinions different from the mainstream were not safe yet....Geographic Reference

item.title: Shahzadpur police station faces tension
item.description: A dispute over a ‘stolen’ motorcycle led to a tense situation at Shahzadpur police station in Sirajganj on Saturday afternoon, with the police and leaders of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal blaming each other for the incident....

item.title: Six killed, two injured by lightning in two dists
item.description: Six people, including a schoolboy, were killed and two others were injured by lightning strikes at different parts of Sunamganj and Habiganj districts in Sylhet division on Saturday noon....

item.title: Accused confesses to killing tea stall owner in Khulna
item.description: Abul Khayer, an accused in the murder case of tea stall owner Din Mohammad at Rupsa in Khulna, has confessed to his involvement in the killing before a Khulna court....

item.title: Harinam Mahajagga of Matuas held in Bagerhat
item.description: A two-day Harinam Mahajagga and grand congregation of Matua devotees concluded at Bangladesh Ashwini Sebashram in Ganga Channa village of Kolatola union under Chitalmari in Bagerhat on Friday....

item.title: PIMS offers support to tackle measles, viral fever outbreak in Bangladesh
item.description: Pacific Institute of Medical Sciences, a multi-super specialty hospital based in Udaipur, Rajasthan, has expressed its willingness to support Bangladesh in combating the ongoing measles and viral fever outbreaks by strengthening technical and healthcare services....

item.title: Expat’s wife stabbed dead, son injured in Bhola
item.description: A 35-year-old woman was killed and her son injured in an attack by some unidentified people at Shibpur under Bhola Sadar in the district early Saturday....

item.title: Solar irrigation saves 7.5m litres of diesel in Rangpur amid fuel crisis
item.description: Amid global energy market volatility triggered by the Iran-Israel-United States tensions and concerns over fuel supply, Bangladesh’s agriculture sector is feeling the strain particularly during the irrigation season, when diesel-dependent farmers face rising costs and uncertainty....

item.title: Kushtia transport workers call off strike after 32 hours
item.description: Bus services in Kushtia resumed on Saturday afternoon after transport workers withdrew a 32-hour strike, following assurances from authorities during a meeting with stakeholders....

item.title: Five accused in Mirpur robbery case arrested
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police on Saturday said that the Mirpur model police and the detective branch of police in a joint drive had unearthed the mystery of a robbery case and arrested five accused at Savar and Ashulia areas in Dhaka....Geographic Reference

item.title: Khulna region reels from severe load-shedding
item.description: Severe load-shedding has gripped the Khulna region as six out of ten power plants have remained closed due to fuel shortage, reducing electricity generation capacity....

item.title: HADI MURDER: Suspected arms supplier Majedul makes ‘confession’
item.description: A Dhaka metropolitan magistrate court on Saturday recorded the confessional statement of Majedul Haque Helal, the alleged supplier of the firearms used in the killing of Inqilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi in December last year....Geographic Reference

item.title: Opposition party creating obstacles: PMO
item.description: Prime Minister’s Office spokesperson Mahdi Amin on Saturday said that the opposition party was creating obstacles to the country’s progress instead of cooperating with the government’s positive initiatives in the past two months....

item.title: Provision of silent expulsion in public exams scrapped
item.description: The education ministry has scrapped the provision of silent expulsion during the public examinations....

item.title: Woes spiral while govt happy with fuel stock
item.description: Consumer woes keep spiraling by the day because of supply shortage of fuel oils from depots and rationing by the filling stations while the government policymakers remain happy with stocks....
